text.title: API Gateway Patterns Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: Is business logic landing in a Kong Lua plugin or Envoy filter?

conclusion.verdict: move logic to a BFF service; reserve plugins for auth, rate-limit, request-tracing, CORS

branch.question: Is rate-limiting per-IP behind a CDN or load balancer?

conclusion.verdict: rate-limit by consumer (API key / user ID) or X-Forwarded-For; never by remote IP

branch.question: Are inbound requests missing X-Request-ID propagation?

conclusion.verdict: generate at ingress if missing; propagate to downstream; include in structured logs

branch.question: Is the gateway timeout ≥ the client timeout?

conclusion.verdict: reduce gateway timeout below client (e.g. 5s gateway < 10s client) and upstream below gateway (2s/hop)

branch.question: Is there one global circuit breaker?

conclusion.verdict: configure circuit breakers per upstream service; one slow service must not kill all routes

branch.question: Are gateway config changes hand-applied without git?

conclusion.verdict: manage via deck/Terraform and GitOps; reject manual `kong reload`

branch.question: Is the system a monolith with one backend?

conclusion.verdict: use direct reverse-proxy with nginx; gateway adds operational overhead for no gain
signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
